title: Organic Juice Maker Suja, Backer Seek $213 Million in US IPO
summary: Suja Life Inc., a maker of organic cold-pressed juices and other nutritional beverages, and one of its backers are looking to raise as much as $213.3 million in a US initial public offering.

title: Rogers Offers Buyout Packages to Staff, Seeking to Cut Costs
summary: Rogers Communications Inc., Canada’s largest wireless company, is making about 10,000 employees eligible for voluntary buyouts as the telecommunications industry deals with major growth and debt challenges.

title: A Warsh-Led Fed Stands to Reignite the Treasuries Steepener Bet
summary: Wall Street strategists say that a Kevin Warsh-led Federal Reserve may jolt the $31 trillion Treasuries market out of its narrow trading ranges, urging investors to position for shorter-dated yields to eventually move lower.

title: Need For 'Clean Beta' with ETFs: F/M Investments CEO
summary: Alex Morris, CEO & CIO of F/M Investments, joins Scarlet Fu and Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Intel Draws $50 Billion of Orders for $6.5 Billion Bond
summary: Intel Corp. attracted about $50 billion of investor orders for a $6.5 billion bond sale, according to people familiar with the matter, as the chipmaker finances its plan to regain full ownership of an Irish plant.

title: Banks to Join BMO-Led $1.8 Billion Debt Deal for Honeywell Spinoff
summary: Bank of Montreal’s investment banking unit is leading a $1.8 billion debt deal supporting the sale of Honeywell International Inc.’s productivity solutions and services business to industrial manufacturer Brady Corp.

title: Shell’s ARC Deal Seen as Win for Mark Carney’s Pro-Oil Pivot
summary: Shell Plc.’s acquisition of Canada’s ARC Resources Ltd. marks a vote of confidence in Prime Minister Mark Carney’s push to expand the nation’s hydrocarbon exports beyond the traditional US market, analysts say.

title: Walmart Plans to Sell Investment-Grade Bonds on Monday
summary: Walmart Inc. raised $4.25 billion from a high-grade bond offering, boosting the size of the deal amid a flood of issuance that was mostly well received.

title: Potential 'GRFT' ETF Outperforms S&P 500
summary: Bloomberg Intelligence's Athanasios Psarofagis joins Scarlet Fu and Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg ETF IQ.". An ETF designed to track White House Policy has been in limbo for more than a year, as exchanges steer clear of what looks like a high-volatility, politically sensitive trade. The potential GRFT ETF is easily outperforming the S&P 500 this year. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Test Your ETF Knowledge In Our IQ Test
summary: Bloomberg's Scarlet Fu puts Katie Greifeld, Athanasios Psarofagis, and Vilana Hajric to the test on "Bloomberg ETF IQ" in this week's edition of "IQ Test." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ETFs Are Building Blocks in Portfolios: Grimba
summary: Valerie Grimba, director of global ETF strategy at RBC Capital Markets joins Scarlet Fu and Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." They discuss how investors are navigating the proliferation of ETFs as they reach record trading volumes in 2026.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Goldman Offering Total Portfolio Solutions: Shupe
summary: Alyson Shupe, managing director and head of global product strategy at Goldman Sachs Asset Management joins Scarlet Fu and Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." Goldman recently closed its acquisition of Innovator Capital Management, an ETF issuer that was among the first to use options ​to determine outcomes or generate income.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting Causes Alarm Ahead of King’s Visit
summary:
The White House will review security at major events involving President Trump — after a gunman targeted the White House Correspondents' Dinner.

Mishal Husain explains the impact on King Charles' state visit. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Rises as Stalled US-Iran Peace Talks Keep Hormuz Strait Shut
summary: Oil rose after efforts to resume peace talks over the Iran war stalled, leaving the Strait of Hormuz almost impassable and prolonging the supply disruption that has roiled global markets.

title: Stocks Hold Near Peak as Busy Earnings Week Starts: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks held near record highs at the start of a week that will feature a raft of megacap earnings and central bank decisions, with traders weighing conflicting signs about progress toward ending the Iran war.

title: Vanguard, Amazon's Alexa Team Up For Proxy Voting
summary: John Galloway, global head of investor engagement at Vanguard , joins Scarlet Fu and Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." They discuss Vanguard's announcement on Monday for a new integration with Amazon's Alexa, making it easier for investors to enroll in their retail proxy voting choice program.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Blue Owl Fund Investors Rebuff Boaz Weinstein Offer to Buy Shares
summary: Investors in a Blue Owl Capital Inc. fund tendered less than 1% of shares to Boaz Weinstein’s Saba Capital Management and Cox Capital Partners, which had offered to buy them out at a steep discount, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Firms Swarm US Market With New Debt, Seizing Issuance Window
summary: Firms swarmed the US primary markets with new debt sales on Monday, seizing a window to lock in borrowing costs ahead of a raft of earnings and central bank decisions, even as uncertainty in the Middle East lingers.

title: Stocks Hold Near Peak as Busy Earnings Week Starts
summary: Stocks held near record highs at the start of a week that will feature a raft of megacap earnings and central bank decisions, with traders weighing conflicting signs about progress toward ending the Iran war. Kristy Akullian, BlackRock Head of iShares Investment Strategy for the Americas joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss the markets and the 2026 Spring Investment Directions Outlook (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Starboard Builds Stake in Flowserve, Push for Changes
summary: Activist investor Starboard Value has built a significant stake in Flowserve Corp. and is speaking to the industrial company about potential changes, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Strong Start For ETFs In 2026, What's Next For The Fed | ETF IQ 4/27/2026
summary: "Bloomberg ETF IQ" focuses on the opportunities, risks and current trends tied to the trillions of dollars in the global exchange traded funds industry. Today's guests: Goldman Sachs Asset Management Head of Global Product Strategy Alyson Shupe, RBC Capital Markets Director of Global ETF Strategy Valerie Grimba, F/M Investments CEO & CIO Alex Morris, and Vanguard Global Head of Investment Engagement John Galloway. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Kalshi Completes First Block Trade, Backed by Jump Trading
summary: Kalshi Inc. said it has completed its first ever bespoke block trade, with Jump Trading providing liquidity for the prediction market exchange.

title: White House to Assess Safety Measures After Dinner Shooting Says Leavitt (Opening Remarks)
summary:
The White House will convene a meeting early this week to discuss safety measures for major events involving President Donald Trump, following Saturday’s shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner that spurred fresh scrutiny of security practices.
The gathering, which will be held by Chief of Staff Susie Wiles, will include the White House operations team, US Secret Service and Department of Homeland Security leadership, according to Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Carney Evokes War Bonds by Inviting Citizens Into Sovereign Fund
summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney’s new sovereign wealth fund will be a rarity: It will invite direct investments from ordinary people.

title: Grupo Mexico to Merge Power Unit With BlackRock-Backed Saavi
summary: Mexican mining and railroad conglomerate Grupo Mexico has struck a deal to merge its electricity generation operations with a BlackRock Inc. unit, creating the country’s largest private power operator.

title: Trump Being ‘Humiliated’ in Iran Talks, German Leader Says
summary:
The US is being “humiliated” by Iranian leaders as President Donald Trump struggles to negotiate an end to the war, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said in comments that risk driving a further wedge in transatlantic relations. 
In unusually candid terms, the German leader said he didn’t see “what strategic exit the Americans are now choosing,” adding that Tehran’s negotiators are proceeding “very skilfully — or indeed very skilfully not negotiating.” Former State Department official Heather Conley joined Bloomberg's Balance of Power and said the US and Iran are stuck in a standoff until one side feels enough economic pain to force a return to the negotiating table.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: DeFi Rescue Hurts Anti-Wall Street Pitch After $10 Billion Run
summary: Decentralized finance is in the midst of the largest coordinated rescue in its history, an effort marked by moral hazard concerns and ad-hoc coordination that sit awkwardly with the sector’s founding pitch as a disruptor of traditional finance.

title: Billionaire’s Bid to Offer Kalshi in Brazil Is Snarled by Ban
summary: Brazil’s decision to block access to top prediction market sites over illegal gambling concerns hits particularly close to home for one of the world’s youngest billionaires, Brazilian Luana Lopes Lara, co-founder of Kalshi.

title: Ackman’s Pershing Square IPO Expected to Raise $5 Billion
summary: The IPO for Bill Ackman's Pershing Square is expected to raise about $5 billion, according to people familiar with the matter. Bloomberg's Bailey Lipschultz reports.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ex-Credit Suisse Bankers Aim to Finance Venezuela Oil Revival
summary: A private-credit firm founded by former Credit Suisse bankers is seeking to finance Venezuela’s oil services sector, as one of the country’s largest energy conferences in decades draws investors back to Caracas.
